title: REVISITING THE AFFAIRE DE LA TOUR DE NESLE Tracy Adams
highlights:
2031-0234
- ISSN: 0083-5897

Buy this Article # Between History and Fiction: Revisiting the Affaire de la Tour de Nesle - DOI: https://doi.org/10.1484/J.VIATOR.1.102710  ...    - Publisher: Brepols
- By: Tracy Adams - Next Article
- Table of Contents
- Previous Article

## Abstract

Abstract The imprisonment for adultery in 1314 of the Burgundian daughters-in-law of Philip IV of France (the Affaire de la Tour de Nesle) reminds us that the “interstitial” positions occupied by royal women were precarious. At first glance, the princesses’ downfall seems the predictable result of their acts. However, chronicle accounts raise questions about their guilt. The Grandes Chroniques de France omits almost entirely the calamitous formation of leagues of angry barons  ...    history, that controlled the lives of medieval royal women. © Brepols Publishers NV
